TEXT: SLMCC UK & COSMOS UK – Sri Lanka Flood Emergency Appeal

Innā lillāhi wa innā ilayhi rājiʿūn

Sri Lanka is facing severe flooding which has caused major destruction. Many lives have been lost, families have been displaced, and essential services have been badly affected. Our brothers and sisters are going through a very difficult time and urgently need our support.

SLMCC UK and Cosmos UK are jointly preparing an urgent response. We are in contact with people on the ground and gathering reliable information to ensure help reaches those most in need.

Community Gathering Tonight Saturday (29.11.2025) After Isha (8.00 PM)
SLMCC UK and Cosmos UK warmly invite all Cosmos UK affiliates and the general public to attend this important community gathering at SLMCC.

The programme will include:
* A short reminder
* Feedback from scholars in Sri Lanka explaining the situation
* A community consultation on how we can assist effectively
* Dinner will be served, InshaAllah

This is a very challenging time for many families. Let us come together as one community and do whatever we can to provide relief and support.

May Allah bring ease, protection, and comfort to all those affected.

Management, SLMCC UK & Cosmos UK
COSMOS UK Update – Sri Lanka Flood Emergency Meeting at SLMCC Harrow

*COSMOS UK Update – Sri Lanka Flood Emergency Meeting at SLMCC Harrow on 29.11.2025*

An urgent community gathering was held at SLMCC, jointly organised by SLMCC UK and COSMOS UK as part of the Sri Lanka Flood Emergency Appeal.

Key points from the programme:

*Muslim Aid Country Director – Br. Faizer Khan* attended the meeting and explained how Muslim Aid responds during major disasters and how they will support relief efforts in this crisis, referencing their extensive past experience.

*Updates from All Ceylon Jamiyathul Ulama (ACJU)*

ACJU leadership joined via Zoom, and messages were shared by:
• As-Sheikh Rizvi Mufti – President, ACJU
• As-Sheikh Abdul Kalik – Vice President, ACJU
• As-Sheikh Arkam Nooramith – General Secretary, ACJU

Ulamas provided firsthand accounts of the situation on the ground, detailed ACJU’s active role in relief operations with Muslim Aid, and reminded the community of the Islamic responsibility to support those in need during this devastating period.

*COSMOS UK Engagements*
*Shakeer Nawas* 
 President of COSMOS UK, explained efforts to raise awareness across non–Sri Lankan organisations, UK charities, media, and others on the Help Sri Lanka Project and Cyclone Ditwa effects in Sri Lanka.
 He highlighted engagement with other Sri Lankan faith groups, the Sri Lankan High Commission in London, and civil society organisations.

Shakeer also briefed the gathering on the efforts of other COSMOS UK affiliates such as Masjid Al Jannah (Slough), SLMC Eastham Mosque, SL Kingsbury Mosque, SLIF (BMS) & Team, GWA, Interbuild, as well as members from the North including SLICAM, SL Bolton Mosques, Leicester SLM members, Masjid Al Furqan - Luton and various WhatsApp groups who are supporting fundraising efforts.
He thanked all contributors and emphasised the importance of continuing support, particularly in rebuilding livelihoods and properties after the relief phase.
*He also highlighted the dedicated direct bank account details shared by the Sri Lanka High Commission in London for donations to the Central Bank of Sri Lanka.*

*Community Q&A – Moderated by Br. Arshath Ibrahim (Trustee, SLMCC)*

A productive Q&A session was held with Mr. Faizer Khan, Mr. Shakeer Nawas, and Mr. Segu Razeen (President, SLMCC), focusing on:
• How the community can assist with fundraising
• How donations will reach those in desperate need
• Ensuring transparency and effective distribution
• Addressing various concerns and questions raised by attendees

The gathering brought together affiliates, community members, and partner organisations to strengthen a unified and effective response to the crisis.

*An overall target of £100,000+ is aimed to be raised by UK organisations in this disaster*
Please use the following link for SLMCC Harrow – Help Sri Lanka Fundraising:

*https://www.slmcc.co.uk/helpSriLanka*

Imam Thalha, Mufti Fahad, Sheikh Ibrahim, and Sheikh Anas from SLMCC also contributed to the event, along with other community leaders and members.

May Allah protect everyone and accept all efforts in this regard.

We thank all those who attended this emergency meeting at very short notice.

*COSMOS UK MEDIA*
16th Anniversary Commemoration of the End of Sri Lanka’s 30-Year Civil War

On 1st June 2025, the Sri Lanka Ex-Service Persons’ Association in the UK (SLESPA-UK) hosted a solemn multi-faith religious ceremony at the London Buddhist Vihara to mark the 16th anniversary of the end of Sri Lanka’s decades-long civil war. The event honoured the sacrifices made during the conflict and invoked blessings for all those affected.

The ceremony united religious dignitaries from Buddhism, Hinduism, Catholicism, and Islam, who offered prayers for lasting peace and prosperity in Sri Lanka. The Most Venerable Bogoda Seelawimala Thero, Chief Sangha Nayake of Great Britain and Chief Incumbent of the London Buddhist Vihara, led the Buddhist observances, which included offerings to the Buddha and the monastic community, followed by the sharing of merit and blessings.

Officials from the Sri Lankan High Commission in London, along with other distinguished guests, were also in attendance.

Representing the Islamic community, Ash Sheikh Abdullah Ibrahim, Imam of the Sri Lanka Muslim Cultural Centre (SLMCC Harrow), delivered a moving address reflecting on the nation's journey toward healing and reconciliation. He acknowledged the profound sacrifices of those affected by the conflict and highlighted the enduring physical and emotional scars borne by many. Emphasising the importance of education, he called for initiatives to inform future generations about the history and lasting consequences of the war.

He urged collective action to rebuild Sri Lanka, grounded in mutual respect and understanding among all communities. His words struck a deep chord with the audience, reinforcing the need for internal and external peace to secure a harmonious future for the nation.

In a poignant moment, Imam Abdullah stated, “Those who sought conflict were consumed by it; those who sought peace can now embrace it.” This sentiment encapsulated the long-term consequences of war and the transformative power of peace. He closed his remarks by stressing the importance of unity and resilience, encouraging continued efforts to nurture coexistence among all Sri Lankan communities.

Also present at the event, representing the Council of Sri Lankan Muslim Organisations (COSMOS UK), was Past President Mr. Liyas Abdul Wahid.
This event reflected the enduring commitment of the Sri Lankan diaspora in the UK to honour the past while working together for a future of reconciliation, mutual respect, and lasting peace.
